title: A Difficult League Decision
article_text: My preference was to join the new league, but the comprehensive rules made me think twice. The incentive to win was a cream-colored trophy, which I really wanted. My opponent had a rapid serve that was hard to return, and I had to resist the urge to cancel the match. Later, I regretted my decision to dismiss his skill, because I lost by a wide margin. The ball landed beneath the net, and I felt a stroke of bad luck. My capability to play well was absolute, but I could not overcome the barrier of my own fear. I wish I had corresponded with my coach before the match. He had told me that the team was stupid for not practicing enough. My twin brother, who was keen on sports, had a clinical approach to training. If I had listened to him, I would have won the league title.
